title: Request Targets, Host Authority, Proxy Trust, and Client Identity · Inference Brief: Express Trust Proxy Function
summary: An origin trusts arbitrary Host and X-Forwarded values, builds reset links from the request, rate-limits the proxy address, and logs raw URLs containing secrets. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts express trust proxy function increment in request authority and trusted-proxy decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Configure trust proxy by explicit addresses or hop logic and derive client, protocol, and host only when every trusted hop overwrites forwarded fields.
Validate canonical host and port against configured authority before routing, link generation, cache keys, redirects, or tenant selection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Express Trust Proxy Function · Theory Express Trust Proxy Function
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 3240 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to derive authority only across an explicit trusted-proxy and host allow-list boundary; keep peak memory below the stated byte ceiling; then reuse an idempotency key with different input and reject the fingerprint mismatch. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Configure trust proxy by explicit addresses or hop logic and derive client, protocol, and host only when every trusted hop overwrites forwarded fields.
